rationale: Implementation of `ddx sync` and `--watch`, allowlist enforcement, no-destructive-flags, structured abort, and doctor integration are all present with tests. Missing: FEAT-NNN-sync.md spec under docs/helix/01-frame/features/ (AC8).

findings.summary: AC8 unmet: no FEAT-NNN-sync.md spec was added under docs/helix/01-frame/features/. The acceptance criteria and standing 'specs first → beads' rule call for the spec to land first.
AC1 met: `ddx sync` runs fetch → stash → merge origin/main → pop → commit DDx paths → push with single retry on non-fast-forward.
AC2 met: `--watch` with configurable `--interval` (default 15m) loops in foreground until context cancel.
AC3 met: stashing/staging restricted to ddxManagedPaths; TestSync_UnrelatedDirtyFilesUntouched and TestSync_CommitsOnlyDDxPaths assert the allowlist.
AC4 met: TestSync_NoForceOrNoVerifyFlags asserts no --force/-f/--no-verify/--force-with-lease/--hard/--delete in any git call.
AC5 met: stash-pop conflict and double push failure both write sync-failure.json and return non-nil error.
AC6 met: doctor reads .ddx/sync-failure.json and surfaces a sync_aborted DiagnosticIssue with timestamp and remediation.
AC7 plausible: implementation uses filepath and pure Go git invocations; no bash-isms or symlinks. CI matrix not verified from the diff.
